sourceMovie.title: Raging Fire
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Raging Fire works as a action movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Infernal Affairs
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Raging Fire clicked for you, Infernal Affairs (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Accident Man
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Raging Fire clicked for you, Accident Man (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Raging Fire?
faq.answer: Try Infernal Affairs, Accident Man, SPL 2: A Time for Consequences—each shares DNA with Raging Fire while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Raging Fire?
faq.answer: Raging Fire (2021) is best described as Action, Crime, Thriller, Adventure, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Raging Fire worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
